comment_text.snp4.6:3.4: Namati is usually rendered with “incline” but I feel something stronger is meant here.
comment_text.snp4.6:6.3: The historical past tense here is unusual. It seems to be an allusion to legendary hermits of the past.
comment_text.snp4.6:10.4: In the suttas, virajjati is a stock term in the process of liberation. It follows nibbidā (“disenchantment”) and precedes vimutti (“freedom”). Being free, the arahant has gone beyond this stage.

root_text.snp4.6:10.4: Na hi so rajjati no virajjatīti.

translation_text.snp4.6:1.2: you die before a hundred years.
translation_text.snp4.6:1.3: Even if you live a little longer,
translation_text.snp4.6:1.4: you still die of old age.
translation_text.snp4.6:2.2: yet there is no such thing as permanent possessions.
translation_text.snp4.6:2.3: Separation is a fact of life; when you see this,
translation_text.snp4.6:2.4: you wouldn’t stay living at home.
translation_text.snp4.6:3.1: Whatever a person thinks of as belonging to them,
translation_text.snp4.6:3.2: that too is given up when they die.
translation_text.snp4.6:3.3: Knowing this, an astute follower of mine
translation_text.snp4.6:3.4: would not be bent on ownership.
translation_text.snp4.6:4.1: Just as, upon awakening, a person does not see
translation_text.snp4.6:4.2: what they encountered in a dream;
translation_text.snp4.6:4.3: so too you do not see your loved ones
translation_text.snp4.6:4.4: when they are dead and gone.
translation_text.snp4.6:5.1: You used to see and hear those folk,
translation_text.snp4.6:5.2: and call them by their name.
translation_text.snp4.6:5.3: Yet the name is all that’s left to tell
translation_text.snp4.6:5.4: of a personage when they’re gone.
translation_text.snp4.6:6.1: Those who are greedy for belongings
translation_text.snp4.6:6.2: don’t give up sorrow, lamentation, and stinginess.
translation_text.snp4.6:6.3: That’s why the sages, seers of sanctuary,
translation_text.snp4.6:7.1: For a mendicant who lives withdrawn,
translation_text.snp4.6:7.4: to not show themselves in a home.
translation_text.snp4.6:8.2: they don’t form likes or dislikes.
translation_text.snp4.6:8.4: slip off them like water from a leaf.
translation_text.snp4.6:9.1: Like a droplet slips from a lotus-leaf,
translation_text.snp4.6:9.2: like water from a lotus flower;
translation_text.snp4.6:9.3: the sage doesn’t cling to that
translation_text.snp4.6:9.4: which is seen or heard or thought.
translation_text.snp4.6:10.1: For the one who is cleansed does not conceive
translation_text.snp4.6:10.2: in terms of things seen, heard, or thought.
translation_text.snp4.6:10.3: They do not wish to be purified by another;
translation_text.snp4.6:10.4: they are neither passionate <j>nor growing dispassioned.

variant_text.snp4.6:2.2: Na hi santi → na hi santā (bj); na hī santi (katthaci)
variant_text.snp4.6:3.3: Etampi viditvā → etaṁ disvāna (bj); etampi viditva (?)
variant_text.snp4.6:6.1: Sokapparidevamaccharaṁ → sokaparidevamaccharaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1); sokaṁ paridevamaccharaṁ (?)
